value.Description: Numeric code identifying the event type in an active visual oddball P3 task. On each trial, one of five uppercase letters (A, B, C, D, or E; Geneva font, 2.5 x 2.5 degrees of visual angle) was presented for 200 ms in the center of the screen over a fixation point, with an ISI of 1200-1400 ms. Participants completed 200 trials across five blocks of 40 trials. In each block, one letter was designated the target (p = .2) and the remaining four were non-targets (p = .2 each; p = .8 combined). Each letter served as the target in exactly one block, with block order randomized across participants. Participants pressed one button for targets and another for non-targets.

Levels.11: Stimulus onset - uppercase letter A (target, p = .2, 200 ms); designated block target: A
Levels.21: Stimulus onset - uppercase letter A (non-target, p = .2, 200 ms); designated block target: B
Levels.31: Stimulus onset - uppercase letter A (non-target, p = .2, 200 ms); designated block target: C
